title: SUPPORT & THERAPY GROUPS - 211info
text: OREGON COMMUNITY PROGRAMS SUPPORT & THERAPY GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers hands-on parenting groups that helps build skills to respond to children's needs and managing challenging behaviors. 1170 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents, resource, foster, adoptive, and kinship caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 743-4340 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 743-4340 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on Lane Transit District downtown loop bus route, corner of 12th and Pearl. Services: Parenting Skills Classes Parenting Skills Classes * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - CONNECTIONS PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAMS MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - INTENSIVE IN-HOME BEHAVIORAL HEALTH TREATMENT MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT GROUP If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
